strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Genuine entrepreneurial experience as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Recent AI-adjacent work at re:cinq building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling, plus a track record of public talks and workshops
concerns: No principal/director-level scope: career history is senior/staff IC delivery roles, with no evidence of setting technical strategy across an organization
No recognized AI expertise — no AI-related advanced degree, publications, or demonstrated AI products/platforms at scale; AI exposure is limited to ~1 year of agent tooling
Cloud experience is practitioner-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than the cloud-platform strategy depth the role centers on

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI-built products

response.justification: The candidate clears the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning low-latency DLang services, TypeScript product work, and infrastructure. The founder role at GISCollective evidences the preferred entrepreneurial trait, and MCP/agent tooling at re:cinq shows emerging AI fluency. However, the role's core competencies — recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products, cloud platform strategy, and director-level influence advising strategic customers — are largely absent from the résumé: there is no advanced AI degree, no AI product shipped at scale, and no role above staff-level IC delivery. The evidence supports a strong senior/staff engineer, not a principal AI strategist.
